title: release_manifest --check joins commands.verify (standing guard)
action: Per review finding arch P2-3: append 'node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --selftest && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check' to .bee/config.json commands.verify so the release manifest guard actually stands (today it runs only as a one-shot cell verify and goes stale silently). Regenerate the stored manifest first (release_manifest --write) so --check is green after p2-1's fsutil change. Depends on p2-1 (manifest must reflect the final tree).

truths: commands.verify includes release_manifest --selftest && --check as the final suite(s)
--selftest passes (checker bites on an injected diff); --check green on the real tree
prohibitions: do not add --check while the stored manifest is stale (regenerate first)

trace.outcome: release_manifest --selftest && --check joined commands.verify (13 suites) — the release-integrity guard now stands and can no longer go stale silently (review finding arch P2-3). Also fixed test_onboard_bee's fixture to vendor templates/lib via readdirSync instead of a 2-file hand-list (crit-pattern 20260714) — exposed when p2-1's onboard fsutil import crashed the curated fixture launcher; removed now-dead REAL_DETECT_SRC.
trace.verify_output: release_manifest --selftest OK (checker bites); --check 32 files match; full 13-suite verify chain EXIT=0 (manifest guard now the final suite); test_onboard_bee fixture derives lib via readdirSync (crit-pattern 20260714 fix) so the shared-import doesn't rot it
